In a true "diptère" (dipteral) temple, there would be two rows of columns surrounding the cella (the inner chamber of the temple). However, in a "pseudo-diptère" temple, there is only one row of columns, but they are spaced in such a way that they give the appearance of a dipteral arrangement. Essentially, the columns are placed at a greater distance from the cella, creating the illusion of an additional row of columns.

This architectural style was used to create a grander and more imposing appearance without the need for the additional columns and structural complexity of a true dipteral temple.
